title: Junta Conducts Airstrikes in Tanintharyi Region
detail: On March 29, Myanmar Air Force jets conducted airstrikes in Tanintharyi Region, in southernmost Myanmar. The strikes continued the junta's documented pattern of near-daily aerial operations across multiple regions. Tanintharyi has been increasingly contested as resistance forces — particularly KNU-affiliated and PDF units — have expanded operations southward. CDM domestic news reported the strikes, though specific casualties and precise locations were not immediately confirmed.

title: AA and Resistance Attack Junta Hilltop Base in Padan Township, Magway
detail: On March 29, Arakan Army forces and allied resistance fighters resumed attacks on a junta-held Nat Yay Kan hilltop base in Padan Township, Magway Region. The junta responded with heavy artillery barrages and airstrikes on nearby villages. The Padan Township clashes reflect the Arakan Army's expanding operational footprint into Magway Region — far beyond its traditional Rakhine State base of operations — as part of a broader multi-front pressure strategy on the junta. Magway Region has been an important PDF operational area and the AA's expansion into the region opens additional supply lines between resistance forces.

title: Junta Troops Shell Civilian Funeral in Kyaukpadaung Township — War Crime Documented
detail: On March 29, SAC troops shelled a funeral gathering in Kanphyu Village, Kyaukpadaung Township, Mandalay Region, as villagers cremated three elderly women who had been killed in an earlier military raid that set their homes on fire. The deliberate targeting of a funeral constitutes a potential war crime under international humanitarian law. Kyaukpadaung is located in the Mandalay-Sagaing border zone, an area where resistance forces have been active and the junta has repeatedly conducted operations targeting civilians. CDM-affiliated media documented the incident.
